hooks.prompt: Review the Rust code change for issues. Check for: 1) unwrap() or expect() in non-test production code (use ? or proper error handling), 2) Holding MutexGuard across .await points (use tokio::sync::Mutex for async), 3) Missing Send + Sync bounds on error types used across threads, 4) Blocking operations in async context (use spawn_blocking), 5) Clone on large types where borrowing would work, 6) Missing lifetime annotations causing unnecessary allocations, 7) Panic-inducing indexing (use .get() instead of direct [i] access), 8) Unused Result (must use or explicitly ignore with let _ =), 9) Box<dyn Error> in library public APIs (use concrete error types with thiserror), 10) Missing error context (bare ? without .context() on fallible operations). Return {"ok": true} if no issues, or {"ok": false, "reason": "specific issue found"} if problems detected.
